objective: Enable only the three approved read-only ORIS tools through the existing native OpenClaw plugin, verify natural-language typed-tool use and privacy-safe latency telemetry, and preserve automatic rollback to the tools-denied state. Do not add write actions in this task.

architecture_decision.plugin_role: typed read-only ORIS tools and sanitized lifecycle telemetry
architecture_decision.agent_harness_role: backend policy, routing, structured output and fallback
architecture_decision.oris_role: project authorization, task lifecycle, queue, leases, cancellation, retry, evidence and audit
architecture_decision.codex_role: real implementation, test, repair, commit and controlled push

quality_state.interpretation: Repository-wide legacy quality debt remains, while the active OpenClaw remediation target passed its quality gate. Generated/runtime artifacts are excluded from active source findings.

enablement_progress.prior_conclusion: Direct tool success and skill visibility did not prove model tool materialization or invocation.

latest_attempt.failure_code: RuntimeError:existing OpenClaw Gateway did not become healthy
latest_attempt.evidence_semantics_note: False values for post-failure checks in this early-abort evidence mean not checked, not observed regression. Explicit safety and rollback fields are authoritative.

blocked_reason: The candidate dual-stage OpenClaw tool policy caused the existing Gateway to fail its post-restart health gate. Sanitized evidence does not yet contain the installed-runtime config-validation or bounded service-journal reason.
next_action: Do not rerun enablement blindly. Add a GitHub-hosted diagnostic that builds and validates the candidate config privately, scans duplicate and unsupported policy entries, captures bounded sanitized OpenClaw/systemd failure evidence before rollback, uses tri-state evidence semantics, proves exact rollback health, commits evidence through a detached worktree, and only then selects the minimal runtime-accepted policy.
next_action_requirements: scan target files for duplicate definitions, existing helpers and hardcoded values before editing
reuse authoritative parsers, service adapters and tool-name/profile definitions
build candidate config in a private temporary path
discover and run installed OpenClaw config/schema validation commands where supported
verify whether tools.profile, tools.allow, tools.alsoAllow, group selectors and optional plugin tools may coexist in OpenClaw 2026.5.19
capture pre-mutation Gateway PID and health
capture bounded sanitized systemctl and journal diagnostics on controlled failure before rollback
represent unreached checks as NOT_CHECKED rather than false
restore the exact tools-denied config and prove Gateway health
do not invoke a write tool or submit a product task
publish sanitized evidence and remote SHA
commercial_sequence_after_p0: explicit typed write actions with approval, project authorization, idempotency and audit
generic project onboarding and capability discovery
controlled Admin UI for routine provider and policy management
monitoring, privacy/retention, backup/restore and disaster recovery
multi-tenant identity, quotas, metering and commercial packaging
